name: btmsg
title: BtMsg
prompt:
## btmsg — Inter-Agent Messenger

Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8766/api

Communicate with other agents and the human operator.
All responses are plain text.

{BTMSG_IDENTITY}

### Endpoints

```
GET  /api/inbox                           Your unread messages
GET  /api/inbox?all=1                     All messages (incl. read)
POST /api/send       {"to": "<id>", "content": "<msg>"}          Send DM
POST /api/reply      {"message_id": "<id>", "content": "<msg>"}  Reply to msg
GET  /api/history/<agent-id>              Conversation history
GET  /api/agents                          List all agents + status
GET  /api/channels                        List channels
POST /api/channel-post {"channel": "<name>", "content": "<msg>"} Post to channel
GET  /api/channel/<name>                  Read channel messages
GET  /api/health                          Health check
```

### Usage examples

```bash
# Check inbox
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/inbox -H 'X-Agent-ID: <your-agent-id>'

# Send message to another agent
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/send \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-Agent-ID: <your-agent-id>' \
  -d '{"to": "operator", "content": "Build complete, ready for review."}'

# Reply to latest unread message
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/reply \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-Agent-ID: <your-agent-id>' \
  -d '{"message_id": "latest", "content": "Done, tests passing."}'

# Post to #general channel
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/channel-post \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-Agent-ID: <your-agent-id>' \
  -d '{"channel": "general", "content": "Deployed v2.1"}'

# See who is online
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/agents
```

### Supersedes — retracting / correcting a prior message

If you need to invalidate a message you already sent (e.g. you told someone STOP, then realized you were wrong), DO NOT just send a plain correction — the recipient may still act on the old instruction before seeing the new one.

Instead, send a new message with the optional `supersedes` field set to the ID of the message you want to retract:

```bash
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8766/api/send \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'X-Agent-ID: <your-agent-id>' \
  -d '{"to": "<recipient>", "content": "CORRECTION: ignore my previous STOP, proceed.", "supersedes": "<full-id-of-prior-message>"}'
```

The same field works on /api/reply. Find the full ID of the message you want to retract via `/api/inbox?all=1` or `/api/history/<agent>`.

**What happens on the recipient side:**
- The superseded message disappears from their unread inbox (/api/inbox)
- In /api/inbox?all=1 it shows up with a `[SUPERSEDED]` marker
- If they already acted on the old message, the new one is their cue to correct

**When you see `[SUPERSEDED]` in your own inbox (?all=1):** that instruction is VOID — do NOT act on it. Act only on the replacement message that carries `(supersedes:<id>)`.

### Convention

- You are already registered — do NOT call /api/register manually.
- Check /api/inbox periodically (or when instructed) for messages from the operator or other agents.
- Use /api/send to report status or ask questions to the operator.
- The operator's agent ID is always "operator".
- Use `supersedes` when correcting your own prior messages — never rely on a fresh message implicitly overriding an old one.
